summary: This page opens Chapter B, "Background," explaining that the reviewed material is foundational and broadly useful for signals and systems. It introduces Section B.1 on complex numbers, discussing their role in the modern number system and the interpretation of the imaginary unit i with i^2 = -1. The page then begins subsection B.1-1, "A Historical Note," with a brief historical perspective on early number systems.

figures.caption: A plot of a damped oscillation, showing a wave whose amplitude decreases over time, bounded by an exponential decay envelope.
